rules_applied: A: Parenthesized character-name leaks in E2 - strip parens, replace with canonical NL form per Q7-Q9 register patterns (formal Ezel form for stage-direction/third-person-narration; casual epithet for emotional/intimate vocative).

C2: Oude Zak deprecation residue - leftover from 2026-04-27 oude-zak-sweep.json where 18 lines were marked pending_disposition; 9 of those still bypass Phase-C apply. Resolved per Q7 pattern: Oude Ezel for formal/reverent-third-person/quotation; Oude for intimate/mournful/casual-peer.
C3: Luie Zak deprecation - same -Zak pattern as Trouwe Zak / Oude Zak; never had its own sweep file. Luie Ezel formal-elegiac (esp. with wijlen prefix); Luie intimate/mournful.

D: Bare EN first-name leaks (Lazy, Sad, Trusty, Hard) - replace with appropriate NL form. Family register Nonkel Sad -> Nonkel Triestigaard (Q8 Uncle pattern). Casual recollection Lazy -> Luie. Intimate-fond Trusty -> Trouwe. Casual peer Hard -> Bikkeharde.
F: Curly U+201C/U+201D double-quotes -> straight (project consistency rule, missed by Phase-C consistency sweep).
C2-extended: Ouwe Zak — Flemish dialectal variant of Oude Zak; same -Zak deprecation. 4 cells in E1.
C-Slimme: Slimme Zak deprecated form for Smart Ass — same -Zak pattern. 1 cell in E3.
C2/C3-extended (all-caps): the Oude Zak / Luie Zak deprecation also affects shouted/all-caps lines (OUDE ZAK, LUIE ZAK). Earlier regex was case-sensitive; case-insensitive rescan caught these.
decision_status: Caught in 2026-04-29 post-Phase-C audit (full-corpus scan against Originals + placeholder + deprecation patterns). 34 cells. Dispositions follow established Q7-Q9 register logic. Re-extended 2026-04-29 with 3 additional residues caught by post-apply verification. Re-extended pt2 2026-04-29 with 5 more cells: 4 Ouwe Zak (Flemish variant my regex missed) + 1 Slimme Zak (new deprecation). Re-extended pt3 2026-04-29 with 3 more cells: 2 OUDE ZAK all-caps (E1_TheProtest) + 1 LUIE ZAK all-caps (E3_LazysGrave). Now 45 corrections total.
